skill.name: Apple HIG: System Experiences
skill.description: Apple Human Interface Guidelines for system experience components including widgets, live activities, notifications, complications, and more.
skill.summary: Provides authoritative Apple HIG guidance for designing system-level experiences across iOS, iPadOS, watchOS, and tvOS platforms.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This skill contains only markdown documentation files with Apple Human Interface Guidelines content. All 1196 static analysis findings are FALSE POSITIVES: URLs are documentation references to developer.apple.com, not network calls; 'C2 keywords' and 'weak crypto' patterns appear in API documentation text, not executable code; 'external_commands' are code examples in markdown, not shell execution. The skill has no executable code, no network capabilities, and poses no security risk.

content.user_title: Design system experiences with Apple HIG
content.value_statement: Designers and developers struggle to create compliant system experiences across Apple platforms. This skill provides authoritative Human Interface Guidelines for widgets, live activities, notifications, complications, and more.
actual_capabilities: Provides Apple HIG guidance for widgets, live activities, and notifications
Explains complication design for watchOS with family-specific layouts
Documents App Clip requirements including size limits and discovery methods
Covers home screen quick actions, top shelf, and watch face design
References official Apple Developer documentation with direct links
Recommends appropriate system surfaces based on use case requirements
limitations: Contains documentation only - does not execute code or build components
References may become outdated as Apple updates HIG - verify with latest docs
Does not provide platform-specific implementation code samples
Limited to system experience components - use other skills for UI controls

use_cases.title: iOS App Widget Design
use_cases.description: Design a home screen widget for a fitness app showing daily activity rings.

use_cases.title: Live Activity Implementation
use_cases.description: Plan a live activity for food delivery tracking from order to arrival.

use_cases.title: Watch Complication Planning
use_cases.description: Design watch face complications for a weather app with multiple sizes.

prompt_templates.title: Widget Design Basics
prompt_templates.prompt: What are the key principles for designing an effective iOS home screen widget? I need to display news headlines.

prompt_templates.title: Live Activity Suitability
prompt_templates.prompt: Should my meditation timer app use a live activity? What are the design requirements for timer-based live activities?

prompt_templates.title: Notification Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a notification strategy for a task management app. What types of notifications are appropriate and how should they be grouped?

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Platform System Experience
prompt_templates.prompt: I'm building a ride-sharing app. Recommend the optimal system experiences for iOS, watchOS, and explain how they should work together for the rider experience.

output_examples.input: How do I design a weather widget?
output_examples.output: For a weather widget, follow these HIG principles: (1) Show glanceable current conditions with high-priority data like temperature and conditions icon; (2) Support multiple sizes - small for current temp only, medium adding forecast, large with detailed hourly view; (3) Update frequency should balance freshness with system budget - typically every 15-30 minutes; (4) Tap should deep-link to the relevant forecast view, not the app root; (5) Use semantic colors that adapt to light/dark mode.

output_examples.input: When should I use App Clips vs the full app?
output_examples.output: Use App Clips when: (1) Users need quick, single-task functionality like paying for parking or renting a bike; (2) Physical location discovery via App Clip Codes, NFC tags, or QR codes; (3) Demo experiences before full app install. App Clips must: complete tasks without requiring full app install, stay under 10MB, load instantly without splash screens, and avoid requiring account creation upfront. The full app is better for: ongoing engagement, complex multi-feature workflows, and scenarios requiring background operations.

best_practices: Design for glanceability - users should understand content in 1-2 seconds
Respect system and update budgets to avoid throttling or disabled updates
Always deep-link to relevant content, never to the app root screen
anti_patterns: Displaying ads or promotional content in widgets or live activities
Sending notifications for purely marketing purposes without user value
Requiring full app install to complete tasks started in an App Clip

faq.question: What is the maximum size for an App Clip?
faq.answer: App Clips must be 10MB or less for cellular download. Keep them as small as possible for faster launch times, especially in low-bandwidth situations.

faq.question: How often can widgets update?
faq.answer: Widget update frequency depends on system budget and widget activity level. Typical ranges are 15-30 minutes for active widgets. The system may throttle updates based on battery and usage patterns.

faq.question: Can live activities appear on the Dynamic Island?
faq.answer: Yes, live activities can appear on Dynamic Island for iPhone 14 Pro and later. Design for both Dynamic Island compact/expanded states and Lock Screen presentation.

faq.question: What are complication families?
faq.answer: Complication families are size categories for watch face complications: modular small/large, circular small/medium/large, graphic corner/rectangular, and utilitarian small/large. Each requires distinct layout designs.

faq.question: Do home screen quick actions require user permission?
faq.answer: No, home screen quick actions (3D Touch/Haptic Touch) are system-managed and appear automatically when users long-press your app icon. No permission is needed.

faq.question: What content works best for tvOS top shelf?
faq.answer: Top shelf should showcase featured content: new episodes, trending items, continue watching, or personalized recommendations. Use high-quality artwork and keep content fresh to encourage engagement.
